column8: The "Classic period" lasted for more than 4000 years.
column9: Archaeologists often have meetings with historians to discuss the Maya civilisation.
column10: Not all of the buildings the Maya started building in the 700s were finished.
column11: Maya buildings were usually very expensive to build.
column12: Around 500AD, the city of Teotihuacan ruled over the Maya civilisation.
column13: Scientists have no idea how much rain fell during the Classic period.
column14: Some Maya cities continued after the end of the Classic period.
column15: Maya people today all speak the same language.
